page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 12 shared vCPUs with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, 12 physical cores, and 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 24.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. This server does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on external network storage options.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing in the bottom 10% at 784.69. However, multi-core scaling is more competitive, with stress-ng multi-core performance (9386.85) and Geekbench multi-core scores (8209.0) both reaching average tiers. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 60.68 GB/sec for uncached large block reads and 40.05 GB/sec for writes. Large language model inference is viable for small 135M models, but drops to a poor 6.21 tokens/sec for 7B model text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-24GB offers a high vCPU count relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU pinning. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which introduces potential resource contention and explains the low single-core benchmark scores. It represents a budget-friendly option for parallel processing tasks where absolute single-thread speed is not critical.

This server is suited for multi-threaded software compilation, parallelized data processing, static web serving, and basic database operations. It is less appropriate for single-threaded applications, memory-intensive databases, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 server featuring 12 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 24.0 GB of RAM. It does not include local storage or a GPU. Performance benchmarks show a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities; single-core stress-ng performance falls into the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core metrics, such as Geekbench multi-core (8209.0) and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (111.71 GB/sec), align with average mid-tier performance. Memory bandwidth remains stable, with uncached large block reads at 60.68 GB/sec. This shared-CPU instance offers a cost-effective option for parallelized workloads, though it presents a tradeoff in single-thread execution speed. It is best suited for multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight parallel processing tasks.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 server with 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering average multi-core performance alongside lower single-core efficiency.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-24GB features 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 24 GB RAM, delivering mid-tier multi-core performance for budget workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, multi-core virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and a balanced compute-to-memory ratio for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 12 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz clock speed
24.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Average multi-core performance contrasted by low single-core efficiency
Well-suited for multi-threaded software compilation and static web serving
